title: Cleaner Air Spaces Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill tells the Environmental Protection Agency to give money to local air agencies to help communities when wildfire smoke makes the air dangerous to breathe. The agencies must work with community groups to set up clean air centers where people can go to breathe cleaner air during wildfires. They also give out air cleaning machines to low-income families and teach people how to make safe clean air rooms in their own homes. The program focuses on helping areas that are most likely to have problems with wildfire smoke, and it must include at least one tribal agency.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Cleaner Air Spaces Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Cleaner Air Space Program Grant
sections.text:
The head of the Environmental Protection Agency (a federal agency that protects air and water quality) must give money grants to local air pollution control agencies. These grants will help create cleaner air space programs in communities. Each grant cannot be more than three million dollars. At least one grant must go to a tribal agency that controls air quality on tribal lands.

To get a grant, an air pollution control agency must send in an application. The application must include a plan for how they will run a cleaner air space program. This plan must show they will work with at least one community organization. The plan must explain what each group will do in the program. It must say which neighborhoods or groups of low income households will get air cleaning machines. It must describe how they will teach people about air cleaning machines and advertise clean air centers. The plan must explain where they will set up a clean air center and how much the program will cost.

Agencies that get grants must work with at least one community organization. They must set up at least one clean air center in an area that might be affected by wildfire smoke. The center must be open to people from low income households and must be open and staffed during wildfire smoke events. They must advertise the clean air center and the program to the public during smoke events. They must give away at least one thousand air cleaning machines to qualifying households for free, along with one replacement filter for each machine. They must give out materials that teach people how to use the machines to create a clean air room in their home.

The agencies must collect information about what types of air cleaning machines they gave away, how many they distributed, and how much each type cost. They must give this information to the Environmental Protection Agency. Within six months of giving someone an air cleaning machine, they must do an anonymous survey asking if the person understood how to set up a clean air room, how often they used the machine, what problems they had, if the air was cleaner in their clean air room, and how the program could be better.

Within three years after this law is passed, the Environmental Protection Agency must report to Congress about all the cleaner air space programs that used these grants. The report must include the names of agencies that got grants, information about the air cleaning machines they distributed, survey responses from people who got machines, and recommendations about whether to expand the program and how to improve it.

This section defines important terms. Administrator means the head of the Environmental Protection Agency. Air pollution control agency means local agencies that control air pollution as defined in the Clean Air Act. Clean air center means one or more clean air rooms in a building the public can access. Clean air room means a room designed to keep harmful air pollution as low as possible during wildfire smoke events. Covered household means a low income household that includes someone at high risk of wildfire smoke who could have health problems from smoke because of health conditions, disability, or age. Eligible air filtration unit means an air cleaning machine that meets specific standards for removing smoke particles and does not create ozone. Low income community has the same meaning as in federal tax law.

Congress approves thirty million dollars for fiscal years two thousand twenty six through two thousand twenty eight to run this program. The Environmental Protection Agency can use up to ten percent of this money for administrative costs.
